dayName: First Day at the Crow's Wing

scenarios.title: The Merchant's Lost Wagon
scenarios.target: recover the salt-merchant's wagon from the brigands camped at the Crow's Ford

contributions.line: Marek fumbles with the iron lock, his brawny hands unsteady from fatigue, but his soldier's grit keeps him trying.
Roselle, crouching in the shadows, inches past the sentry with her quick feet and cautious eyes, avoiding any noise.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Despite their efforts, the lock proves stubborn and the sentry's alertness is too sharp; their infiltration is thwarted, leaving the wagon still out of reach.

scenarios.title: The Brigand's Tongue
scenarios.target: wring the location of the bandit cache out of the prisoner before dawn

contributions.line: Marek's brawny fist presses into the prisoner's collar, demanding the truth with a soldier's grit.
Veska's sharp eyes catch inconsistencies in the prisoner’s story, her calm tone probing for slips.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Marek's physical intimidation rattles the prisoner enough to crack his false calm, while Veska's keen reading exposes contradictions; together, they force the bandit's secret from him.

scenarios.title: Watchtower at the Pass
scenarios.target: commission a new watchtower at the southern pass before winter closes the road

contributions.line: Veska meticulously drafts the structure, her sharp eye catching flaws in the timber before they become a problem.
Marek, despite fatigue darkening his eyes, keeps a wary watch over the crew, his brawny frame tense and alert.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Veska's precise planning prevents major flaws in the design, but Marek's strained oversight fails to rally the crew, leading to a half-finished tower that won't hold through the spring melt.
